title: Scholar's Strike
setup: White's queen on **h5** and bishop on **c4** both stare at the weakest square in Black's camp.
hint: Both pieces aim at {{f7}} — only the king defends it.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the classic **Scholar's Mate** finishing move. The queen and bishop have built a battery against {{f7}}, the only square in Black's opening position guarded solely by the king.

Play {{Qxf7#}}. The queen captures the pawn and gives check, defended by the bishop on **c4**.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{e7}} — blocked by Black's own pawn
- {{e8}} and {{d8}} — covered by the queen herself from f7
- Capturing the queen with {{Kxf7}} is illegal because the bishop on **c4** controls f7
- No piece can interpose: f7 is adjacent to the king

> **Takeaway:** Always check whether f7 (or f2 for Black) is defended only by the king before developing. A queen + bishop battery on the a2-g8 diagonal is one of the oldest tricks in chess.
